objective: Three changes: 1) In glitchlab/config.yaml, add auditor: "openai/gpt-5.4-mini" to the routing block alongside the existing agent routes. 2) In glitchlab/auditor/task_writer.py, change role="planner" to role="auditor" in the router.complete() call inside the write_tasks method. 3) In glitchlab/router.py, find the role_limits dict and add "auditor": 0.75 so the auditor gets its own token budget separate from the planner. Only touch these three files. Verify: python -m ruff check glitchlab/config_loader.py glitchlab/auditor/task_writer.py glitchlab/router.py && python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
commit_message: chore: add dedicated auditor routing and budget
implementation_summary: Updated only the requested files: added `auditor: "openai/gpt-5.4-mini"` to `glitchlab/config.yaml`, changed the `router.complete()` call in `glitchlab/auditor/task_writer.py` to use `role="auditor"`, and set auditor's `role_limits` entry to `0.75` in `glitchlab/router.py`. Verification: Ruff passed, but the requested pytest run failed due to an unrelated existing failure in `tests/agents/test_implementer_run_check_success_message.py` (`TypeError` in `glitchlab/agents/implementer.py`), which is outside the allowed files.

data.description: Verifies the auditor task writer calls the router using the public auditor role and that the router can resolve the configured auditor model, catching regressions in both the task writer role change and auditor routing config.
